cards.back: Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTP) is a widely used protocol for communication between IoT devices and servers.
Constrained Application Protocol (CoAP) is a lightweight, binary, and RESTful protocol designed for constrained networks and devices.
Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) is a lightweight, publish-subscribe-based protocol for IoT communication and data exchange.
WebSockets is a protocol that enables bi-directional, real-time communication between web clients and servers, suitable for IoT applications.
Lightweight Machine-to-Machine (LWM2M) is a standardized protocol for IoT device management and data exchange, targeting constrained networks and devices.
